ending: Tala closes the volume as a Refined mage who is relocating her base from Banfast to the city where Rain is based. Her iron bond has been joined to her soulbound weapon, giving her formidable defenses with limits that Cazor continues to test. Terry remains beside her to explore a flock partnership, without accepting a soul bond. Simon, Petra, and their family are building a working home within Kit, though internal teleportation remains unsolved. Tala has renewed ties with her siblings and watched the twins leave for the academy, but she gives no answer to her father's offer of future support. Banfast still holds Rob and five founts, Tala retains an inactive automaton for study, and the nature of founts, the dome, void, and the next world remains unsettled. An upland tiger also survives in its valley after driving Tala and Terry away. Tala retains friends and collaborators in Banfast and plans to visit them after establishing herself in her new base.
in_short: Tala returns to human territory carrying an iron Doskanok that cannot be removed, so she negotiates a soul bond with it. Back in Banfast, she shares intelligence from captivity, confronts and integrates the false identity imposed on her, ends her Caravan Guild obligations, and prepares for refinement. Holly helps merge the unstable iron bond into Tala's weapon, and Tala survives a painful transformation into a Refined mage. Kit becomes a staffed mobile home for Simon, Petra, and their family. Tala later witnesses a mage become a fount and sends it onward through a dangerous encounter spanning Zeme, a mysterious dome, and the next world. She reconnects with her siblings in Marleyweather, leaves reconciliation with her parents unresolved, and supports the academy-bound twins' departure. Terry chooses to remain and attempt a true flock partnership. After retreating from an upland tiger, Tala returns to Banfast, tests her iron defenses with Cazor, and prepares to relocate to the city where Rain is based.

recaps.text: Previously: Tala is a human mage who travels with Terry, an intelligent terror-bird capable of crossing space in sudden flickers. She also keeps Kit, a named dimensional refuge that carries her possessions and serves as a mobile shelter. Their unusual partnership and Tala's accumulated magical bonds shape the condition in which she begins this volume.

Tala reaches a human city with an iron Doskanok threaded through her body and asks a human paragon to remove it through a repeating teleportation. The process burns away her exterior iron and strips her inscriptions, but the creature resists separation and begins forcing a bond. Tala rejects the destructive selves it offers and negotiates control over her own iron, power, and identity. She survives with the Doskanok soulbound to her, then restores her inscriptions from Kit. The bond lets her claim iron she owns, draw it into her body, and reinforce her magic, but it also affects nearby mundanes and produces a severe hunger. Tala tells the paragon about her abduction, erased memories, service to the House of Blood, and escape after killing her controller. Rain reunites with her, and they leave for Banfast with Terry and the paragon. Illie and Ingrit learn that Tala is alive. During the journey Tala tries sealing herself in bonded iron, causing catastrophic magical overload and internal injury. She survives, but any deeper union with the iron must wait until refinement and careful testing.

Tala discovers that repeated errors in her regenerated body will eventually become fatal unless she refines or finds another repair. Grediv brings her and Rain into Banfast, where Lynn, Ingrit, Holly, and other specialists receive her. A tiered briefing confirms her captivity, her forced service as a House of Blood enforcer, and the collar attack that injected the iron Doskanok after she killed her controller. Tala also discloses recovered automata materials, arcane holds, and the uncertain status of founts. She reveals that Kit is a living dimension able to consume other spaces, and Banfast accepts responsibility for Rob and five founts while deciding their treatment. Constructionists examine Kit and find it damaged, then attempt to reinforce it. Holly prevents Tala from using a reinscribing device before refinement and begins coordinating a safe preparation plan. Tala resumes exercise, iron practice, and ordinary social contact while experts study how to stabilize her altered body. When constructionists open crates of reinforcement materials around Kit, the sanctum begins an uncontrolled void-powered reaction. The chapter closes with the attempted reinforcement still a dimensional emergency.

Kit absorbs magical containment crates and reproduces their stabilizing structures, convincing Constructionist Guild examiners that the sanctum is a living being able to incorporate magical methods. Plans emerge for researchers or a household to work inside it, though teleportation and safety remain unresolved. Ingrit converts demand for Tala's arcane knowledge into money, favors, and expert access. Tala pays her entire debt, an emotional release tied to the circumstances of her capture. Holly updates her inscriptions and keeps refinement on a cautious schedule while Tala rebuilds her strength and learns finer control of her internal iron. Research connects Tala's void sight to her soulbound weapon, and Kit demonstrates that it can relocate a consenting person within itself. Tala begins trauma therapy and recounts her capture, enforced identity, and return. Her therapist urges her to stop isolating the false persona created during captivity. With Alat's help, Tala confronts that construct, forgives it as another victim of manipulation, and dismisses it. The mental partition collapses, leaving Tala to experience the contained memories and emotions directly. Integration has begun, but its immediate cost is severe.

Recovered arcane knowledge produces both promising research and a fatal illegal automaton experiment. Tala completes the integration of her imposed captive identity, then works with Alat on fully sensory simulated training. She upgrades her archive link for effectively unlimited ordinary range and, with Lynn, invokes an incapacity clause that removes all fifty-one remaining Caravan Guild trips. Free of that obligation, Tala begins planning a mobile home and a life chosen on her own terms. Lynn advances to Fused. An expert Archon explains theories about reality beasts, Zeme, the fractured-reality region, void magic, and why human and arcane powers remain in an uneasy balance. Cazor then tests Tala's control of owned iron. Within her aura it behaves like part of her, but iron dragged beyond that boundary ceases to be hers and tears at the bond. Holly finds the test exposed dangerous damage. Rather than merging the Doskanok further into Tala's body, specialists join its bond to her soulbound weapon. Tala selects a configuration that leaves her body unchanged, lets the weapon supply protective iron, and allows lost iron to be consumed by void instead of violently relinquished. The merger succeeds and clears a major obstacle to refinement.

After the weapon and Doskanok bond settle, Tala enters Holly's irreversible refining apparatus. The transformation succeeds, removing conceptual corruption, but the pain and confinement leave Tala weak and traumatized. Holly confirms that Tala is now Refined, with greater magical capacity, physical ability, resistance to disease and poison, and no ordinary age-related decline. Advancement toward Paragon will require a change in her mortal perspective that she does not yet understand. While recovering, Tala meets Simon and Petra, a married pair considering work inside Kit with their children. Lynn learns of Tala's refinement and shares the unresolved loss of her own family. A Constructionist Guild specialist designs a morphic sheath so Tala can safely spar with her weapon's new iron-claiming effect. That effect is tied to intended cutting contact and could also emerge through sharp iron formed from Tala's body. Before leaving Banfast, Tala encounters a siphon attacking a building. Another Archon clears civilians while Tala kills the creature by using her weapon's conceptual authority over iron. The victory proves the merged weapon effective, even as Tala remains cautious about its hazards and her unfinished recovery.

Tala disposes of the siphon's corpse and compensates the other Archon who helped rescue civilians. Simon and Petra test life inside Kit with their children, eventually committing to work there once communications, exits, and housing are secure. Petra begins developing meals from magical harvests, while Simon installs an archive interlink and plans durable infrastructure. Tala hires Lynn and her mageling to sell mundane arcane goods and builds reserves for future travel. The Constructionist Guild offers a recovery net that will return Kit to the Guild if Tala dies, in exchange for inheriting the sanctum and its contents. Tala accepts after consulting trusted advisers, protecting Simon and Petra's family from being stranded. Kit receives the recovery net and a grid node, but internal teleportation continues to fail. Petra records her food methods so the work can continue without her. Holly's final assessments clear Tala for unrestricted activity. Tala immediately spars with Rain and finds that refinement has made her much stronger, while Rain's ability to redirect kinetic vectors again makes him a difficult opponent. The first hint of green in Tala's aura also marks a small, uncertain step toward Paragon.

Tala prepares to leave Banfast, speaks with Rain about the burden of her past killings, and demonstrates the reinscriber for Holly. She then travels overland with Terry while Simon, Petra, and their family remain inside Kit. A full iron sheath now reinforces Tala's inscriptions and contains her magical presence, giving her far greater strength and speed when carefully controlled. On the road she finds an elderly mage irreversibly becoming a fount. Tala and Simon record the transformation as the man's body disappears and a flame fount forms. Through void sight, Tala sees a structure of nothingness restraining the fount from moving onward. Her attempt to close it shifts her perception among Zeme, a mysterious dome, and a next world of pure magic that injures her. Tala chooses to send the fount onward rather than claim or stabilize it. The fount vanishes, and Alat's archive record restores memories Tala briefly loses. A specialist can offer only theories about reality, void, magic, and the dome. After recovering, Tala resumes the journey and reaches Marleyweather, where her estranged father is waiting outside the family alchemist shop.

Tala's father asks her to discourage two younger siblings from entering the academy and accepts responsibility for past decisions that harmed her. Tala refuses to deceive the twins. She gathers her siblings inside Kit, makes clear that she still claims them as family, and maintains her separation from her parents' household. Her eldest sister is studying magical theory with a healer-mage and developing an iron-paint business; she later chooses him as her prospective master. Tala spends weeks with her siblings and supports the twins' decision after giving them a realistic account of magehood. She also begins mapping the inactive mature automaton retained in Kit. Terry, carrying grief from the loss of his own flock, subjects Tala to a serious spar meant to decide their future. Tala develops a persistent working that makes local reality harder to manipulate, but Terry defeats her. The twins leave together for the academy. Terry then explains that he has chosen to stay and test whether he and Tala can become a flock, without accepting a soul bond. Simon begins monitoring Tala's unexplained reality-stabilizing working. Tala leaves Marleyweather without resolving whether she will accept her father's offer of future support.

While traveling back toward Banfast, Tala accepts a commission to inspect a mountain valley containing authorized founts. An upland tiger attacks and proves able to draw immense power from the terrain and regenerate major injuries almost instantly. Tala cannot use her most destructive options without violating the commission's limits, and a new iron defense demands too much concentration under the tiger's attacks. Terry joins the fight, then signals retreat when their efforts fail. Tala trusts him and withdraws, leaving the tiger alive for another Archon to address. In Banfast, Tala shares a farewell dinner with Lynn and Lynn's mageling before moving her base to the city where Rain is based. She plans to maintain those friendships and return after several months. Cazor meets her in a reinforced arena for another iron test. His opposed magnetic poles can pull surface iron away when Tala concentrates only on holding it, exposing a limit in her defense. Tala answers by extending her aura through iron tendrils and crushing the poles. She ends the book Refined, free of her caravan obligations, partnered with Terry as a prospective flock, connected to her human allies, and relocating while major questions about founts, Kit, her iron bond, and Paragon advancement remain open.
